parentQuestion: An infant born after prolonged labor with the arm pulled forcefully during deliv...

scaffolds.q: Intrinsic hand muscles control fine finger movements and prevent claw hand.
scaffolds.explain: The intrinsic hand muscles (lumbricals, interossei) flex the MCP joints and extend the IP joints — preventing the claw hand deformity. They're primarily innervated by the ulnar nerve (C8-T1). Lose these muscles, and the extrinsic finger flexors (FDP, FDS) overpower the extensors — the fingers curl into a claw. imagine the delicate balance of opposing forces, and what happens when one side is paralyzed...

scaffolds.q: Lower brachial plexus injuries (C8-T1) can occur during difficult births with the arm pulled upward.
scaffolds.explain: When the infant's arm is forcefully pulled upward during delivery — often in breech presentations with the arm overhead — the lower brachial plexus roots (C8-T1) are stretched beyond their limit. Some fibers tear. Some are avulsed from the spinal cord. Feel the weight of this:drawn into the mechanism — excessive traction, delicate nerve roots tearing. Often permanent. Devastating, isn't it?
